signal_title: UK's Accession to the WTO E-Commerce Joint Initiative
risk_rationale: The UK's accession to the WTO E-Commerce Joint Initiative introduces requirements for digitalization of customs documents and recognition of electronic documents and signatures. This aligns with global trends but requires updates to existing processes.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Currency conversion transparency with real-time FX disclosure at checkout
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Digitalization of customs documents and processes
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The baseline does not explicitly cover digitalization of customs documents, which may require integration with new digital customs systems.

compliance_gaps.baseline_requirement: Return policy minimum of 7-day no-questions-asked (14 in EU)
compliance_gaps.regulatory_requirement: Recognition of electronic documents and signatures
compliance_gaps.gap_description: The baseline does not explicitly address the recognition of electronic documents and signatures in the context of e-commerce transactions.

remediations.title: Integrate Digital Customs Systems
remediations.description: Develop and integrate systems to handle digital customs documents and processes in compliance with the WTO E-Commerce Joint Initiative.
acceptance_criteria: Successful integration with digital customs systems
Compliance with WTO E-Commerce Joint Initiative requirements

remediations.title: Implement Electronic Document Recognition
remediations.description: Update systems to recognize and process electronic documents and signatures as part of e-commerce transactions.
acceptance_criteria: Ability to process electronic documents and signatures
Compliance with WTO E-Commerce Joint Initiative requirements

business_impact: The changes may require significant updates to e-commerce systems, potentially increasing operational costs and requiring training for staff. However, they also present opportunities for streamlined operations and faster processing times.
recommended_actions: Conduct a gap analysis to identify specific system changes needed
Develop a project plan for integrating digital customs systems
Update training materials and conduct staff training on new processes
